# supabase_vault_secret (Resource)

Vault Secret resource for storing encrypted secrets using Supabase Vault

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "supabase_vault_secret" "example" {
  project_ref = "mayuaycdtijbctgqbycg"
  value       = "my-secret-api-key-12345"
  name        = "api_key"
  description = "Third-party API key"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the secret
- `project_ref` (String) Project reference ID
- `value` (String, Sensitive) The secret value to be encrypted and stored

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the secret

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID identifier for the vault secret

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Vault secrets can be imported using the project reference and the secret's UUID,
# separated by a colon ':'.
#
# - project_ref: Found in the Supabase dashboard under Project Settings -> General,
#   or in the project's URL: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/<project_ref>
# - secret_id: UUID of the vault secret (obtained from vault.secrets table)
terraform import supabase_vault_secret.example "mayuaycdtijbctgqbycg:a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
```

## Security Considerations

- The `value` field is marked as sensitive and will not appear in Terraform logs or console output
- Secrets are encrypted at rest using Supabase Vault's encryption
- SQL queries are protected against SQL injection through proper escaping
- Ensure your Terraform state file is stored securely as it contains sensitive data
